issue: Homepage promotes the January 31, 2026 banquet as a latest event after it occurred.
evidence: Homepage latest-events section links the 2026 banquet.
recommended_disposition: Archive the banquet and remove active registration framing.

issue: Banquet Time renders as '31, 2026'.
evidence: Rendered Event Details show Date: Saturday, January 31, 2026; Time: 31, 2026.
recommended_disposition: Correct from approved source before migration; do not preserve this field.

issue: Past banquet still exposes two active-looking Eventbrite Register Now links.
evidence: Both CTAs point to Eventbrite event 1948078227419.
recommended_disposition: Remove registration CTA and retain an archive page.

issue: Expired countdown renders 0 days, hours, minutes, and seconds.
evidence: Rendered page shows four zero counters.
recommended_disposition: Replace with archive state.

issue: Public comment submission form is enabled on an event registration/archive page.
evidence: Form posts to wp-comments-post.php.
recommended_disposition: Do not migrate comments or comment form unless the board explicitly approves moderation.

issue: Primary golf registration exits to Eventbrite.
evidence: Two Register Now CTAs point to Eventbrite event 1985644019715.
recommended_disposition: Capture Eventbrite form/history, then replace in later native-registration phase.

issue: Raffle, mulligan, and donation purchases use three separate Stripe-hosted links outside registration.
evidence: Two buy.stripe.com links and one donate.stripe.com link are rendered.
recommended_disposition: Preserve product evidence; consolidate only in later payment implementation.

issue: A sponsor inquiry is handled in public comments and answered with a personal email address.
evidence: Public comment asks how to buy a hole sponsorship/foursome; reply directs the visitor to a personal MSN address.
recommended_disposition: Create board-approved sponsor packages/contact funnel later; remove this pattern from migration.

issue: Public comments are functioning as an unstructured sponsor support channel.
evidence: Two public responses and a comment submission form are visible.
recommended_disposition: Do not migrate comments; preserve required sponsor lead information separately.

issue: Golf countdown renders all zeros before the June 27 event date.
evidence: Rendered page shows 0 days/hours/minutes/seconds during validation.
recommended_disposition: Fix event-state logic in a later implementation; do not copy current widget.

issue: Events page thanks sponsors/donors but offers no sponsor package or sponsor inquiry CTA.
evidence: Only generic thanks and Donate Now are rendered.
recommended_disposition: Obtain board-approved sponsor packages before building sponsor funnel.

issue: Events listing advertises comment counts, drawing attention away from registration and sponsorship.
evidence: 2026 golf card displays '2 COMMENTS'.
recommended_disposition: Remove comment metadata in future migration.

issue: One-time and monthly donation CTAs leave the site for Stripe-hosted Payment Links.
evidence: Donate Once and Give Monthly point to donate.stripe.com.
recommended_disposition: Retain as current payment evidence; validate later donation architecture separately.

issue: Donation page lacks EIN, board/stewardship detail, quantified impact, and explicit tax-receipt expectations.
evidence: Page has generic nonprofit and tax-deductibility language only.
recommended_disposition: Board/legal review before Ad Grants or donation-funnel implementation.

issue: History-preservation bullet is duplicated.
evidence: The same preservation statement appears twice in Donations directly support list.
recommended_disposition: Deduplicate during content approval.

issue: Contact page combines contact and newsletter subscription forms without a focused campaign conversion.
evidence: Rendered page includes Send Message and Subscribe forms.
recommended_disposition: Separate campaign intent in future landing pages.

issue: Public Login account-modal link appears sitewide without a documented public account purpose.
evidence: Observed on homepage, events, banquet, golf, donate, contact, inductee archive, and inductee detail page.
recommended_disposition: Do not migrate public login UI unless a board-approved member feature exists.

issue: Homepage is broad and mixes mission, inductees, current/past events, and donation CTAs.
evidence: No single campaign intent or conversion path.
recommended_disposition: Not suitable as primary Ad Grants landing page without focused variants.

issue: Events page mixes current and archived content and lacks direct native conversion.
evidence: Registration exits to Eventbrite and sponsorship has no CTA.
recommended_disposition: Poor Ad Grants landing page until event-specific funnels exist.

issue: Past event page has broken time data and stale registration.
evidence: Expired date, malformed Time, active-looking Eventbrite CTAs.
recommended_disposition: Never use as an Ad Grants landing page in current state.

issue: Golf page fragments registration, add-ons, donations, and sponsorship across external/manual channels.
evidence: Eventbrite, multiple Stripe links, and sponsor handling through comments.
recommended_disposition: Poor Ad Grants landing page until one measurable funnel exists.

issue: Donation page has a clear ask but insufficient trust/impact proof for paid acquisition.
evidence: No EIN, board/stewardship details, or impact metrics.
recommended_disposition: Board-review trust content before paid campaign use.

issue: Generic contact/newsletter page lacks campaign-specific content and conversion definition.
evidence: Two unrelated forms share the page.
recommended_disposition: Use focused landing pages instead.
